evidence: spec.md Out of scope section: 'Wiki-export indexing kickoff from scripts/onboard.sh (deferred; wiki-export is a Claude Code skill...)'
Edge Cases 'Theme symlink with existing real file' section: 'The new theme stage MUST call link_file (not raw ln -sf) for every theme target — explicit requirement to preserve user data.'

Scope: '--no-install flag — bypasses ALL detection and enforcement (true CI escape hatch)'. Edge Cases tier-split table: '--no-install passed (any tool state): Skip install stage entirely. Symlinks still run.'
Acceptance case 1 rewritten: 'Idempotency under repeat runs (state-based, not byte-based)' with no-duplicate-symlinks find/sort/uniq -d, sentinel block grep-c <= 1, key-message grep assertions.

New Acceptance case 3a: 'Fresh-Mac happy path (NEW — separates happy path from hard-stop)' — pre-stage with brew+claude+git+python3 present, RECOMMENDED missing, pipe Y, assert brew bundle invoked + symlinks created + onboard panel + exit 0.

Acceptance preamble: 'Mocking strategy: bash function-shadowing (has_cmd() { return 1; } defined in the test before sourcing install.sh) — no PATH manipulation, no Docker.' Integration section: 'Tests use bash function-shadowing (has_cmd() { return 1; }) to mock it without modifying production code.'

Revisions section v1.1: 'Scope cut: dropped wiki-export indexing... Kept theme baseline + cmux (Justin's owner-favored extras).' Out of Scope explicitly retains theme + cmux as in-scope; user made the call to keep them at refine time (decision 1b: cut wiki only).
New 'v0.4.x → v0.5.0 adopter (upgrade flow)' UX section + Edge Cases 'v0.4.x → v0.5.0 migration' section with PRIOR_INSTALL detection + print_upgrade_message + Acceptance case 8.
New flags --non-interactive, --no-onboard, --force-onboard added with auto-detect via [ -t 0 ]. New Acceptance case 9 with 9a/9b/9c sub-cases. onboard.sh outline gates each prompt on [ -t 0 ].

Edge Cases 'set -euo pipefail + brew bundle' section shows the exact required pattern: 'if ! brew bundle --file=$REPO_DIR/Brewfile install; then ... exit 1; fi'. Spec text marks it 'CORRECT pattern (spec requires this form, not implicit error handling)'.
Data & State '"Loud notice" definition' subsection pins glyph (⚠), stream (stderr), format string, repeat (once at decline-time, not at exit), exit code (0 — RECOMMENDED-missing is non-fatal).
Integration 'Owner detection (hardened)' section: detect_owner() function with realpath via cd ... pwd -P, git rev-parse --show-toplevel comparison, MONSTERFLOW_OWNER=1 env override.
Scope: 'Default theme behavior: owner = install without prompt; adopter = prompt-default-N'. Acceptance case 6a: 'theme symlinks created without any read prompt'. No remaining contradiction.
Edge Cases 'Linux user' section + Scope line 'Linux fail-fast guard: top of install.sh exits 1 with macOS-only message'. Acceptance non-test bar lists 'Linux guard verified manually'.
Edge Cases 'SIGINT mid-install' section: cleanup_partial() function + trap cleanup_partial INT TERM + atomic-write .monsterflow.tmp suffix convention.

Approach section closing line: 'Per Codex: "additive surgery framing understates the control-flow changes" — /plan will explicitly diagram new vs old flow to avoid sequencing/early-exit bugs around existing validation/plugin prompts.'

Acceptance non-test bar: 'Onboard panel substring assertions. Test 7 (or a new dedicated test) asserts the panel contains literal /flow, /spec, dashboard/index.html. Adding/removing a numbered step requires a test update.'

Integration: 'The helper does pip3-vs-pip auto-detection at call time (no migration, just resolution). Sister scripts continuing to hardcode pip3 is OUT OF SCOPE for this spec.' Out of Scope: 'Adopting the python_pip auto-detect helper in sister scripts... is a separate sweep, not a migration.'

Data flag table row: '--no-theme: Force theme install off; **wins over --install-theme**'. Edge Cases section '--install-theme + --no-theme passed together' explicitly states '--no-theme wins'. New --no-onboard flag also added.
Data Brewfile block: 'cask "cmux" # Homebrew cask (not a formula); auto-updates per cask author'. Removed prior 'homebrew-cask formula' phrasing.
